title: centaurea scabiosa subsp. sadleriana
intro: Centaurea scabiosa subsp. sadleriana, also known as the Kentish knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Centaurea. Native to the UK and Europe, it is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea scabiosa subsp. sadleriana include its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as its use in treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in tea, tincture, or oil form, but it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
